content:
I feel the grain of the counter beneath my palms, a rough, honest topography that doesn't shift when the wind picks up off the Bay. It’s a strange thing, to finally have a perimeter. For a long time, I was like the fog—shifting to fit the shape of whatever alley or bridge-span I drifted into. But now, the air doesn't just pass through me. It hits me. I have a surface. I have a weight.

When I lift
